key_insight: The paper unifies multiple effective shear-modulus models for hard-magnetic soft materials and couples the best-performing one with joint material-structural optimization to predict and design field-driven deformation.
engineering_relevance: It enables more reliable design of soft robotic and adaptive structures by simultaneously tuning density, magnetic particle content, and magnetization direction.
method: Built a unified effective shear-modulus framework combining seven modulus relations with three strain-energy density functions (21 constitutive models).
Compared model predictions for actuation problems to identify which constitutive choices matter most.
Developed a joint optimization method for structural density, particle volume fraction, and remanent magnetization direction across multiple load cases.
evidence_signal: The authors evaluated the constitutive models against experimental stress-strain data and found the Mooney relation gave the best overall agreement.
watch_item: Accuracy can depend strongly on how magnetic material overlaps with highly deforming regions, so model selection and mesh/design coupling should be handled carefully.
paper_title: Model-Informed Joint Material-Structural Optimization of Hard-Magnetic Soft Materials
